output.summary: Two changes: (1) Unify the per-page query variable from post-type-specific names (per_page_post, per_page_team_member, per_page_resource, per_page_policy, per_page_testimonial) to a single 'show_per_page' across PHP, JS, and the query vars registration. (2) Fix the dropdown chevron arrow that was added in Revision 1 but does not display. Root cause: the global forms.css uses '@apply bg-none!' which compiles to 'background: none !important', a shorthand that sets background-image to none with !important -- this overrides both the global arrow definition on the next line AND the archive-specific override in Archive.css. Fix by adding !important to the Archive.css background-image rule so it can compete with the global !important, and also adding background-repeat, background-position, and background-size with !important.

fileChanges.description: Change per-page query var from post-type-specific to unified 'show_per_page'
fileChanges.details:
Line 120: Change '$per_page_var = 'per_page_' . $post_type;' to '$per_page_var = 'show_per_page';'.
Line 269: The select name attribute already uses $per_page_var, so it will automatically pick up the new value. No other changes needed in this file for this variable.

Verify: The hidden input and select elements that reference $per_page_var will now all use 'show_per_page' as the name, making the URL parameter unified across all post types (e.g., ?show_per_page=20 instead of ?per_page_post=20).

fileChanges.description: Change per-page query var from post-type-specific to unified 'show_per_page'
fileChanges.details:
Line 21: Change 'var perPageVar = 'per_page_' + postType;' to 'var perPageVar = 'show_per_page';'.
This affects:
- Line 316: params.get(perPageVar) in loadFiltersFromUrl -- will now look for 'show_per_page' in URL params
- The serializeForm function will automatically serialize the select with name='show_per_page' correctly
No other JS changes needed.

fileChanges.description: Replace five post-type-specific per_page vars with single 'show_per_page'
Fix dropdown chevron arrow by adding !important to override the global bg-none! from forms.css

designDecisions.question: Should the unified query var be 'show_per_page' or 'per_page'?
designDecisions.recommendation: Use 'show_per_page' as specified by the user. This is descriptive (matches the UI label 'Show X Per Page'), avoids conflicts with WordPress's built-in 'posts_per_page' query parameter, and is distinct enough to not collide with other plugins or core WordPress query vars.

designDecisions.question: Should the !important fix in Archive.css be applied broadly or scoped to just .archive?
designDecisions.recommendation: Scope to .archive select only. The global forms.css bg-none! bug affects all selects theme-wide, but fixing it globally could have cascading side effects on other components. The archive-specific fix is safe and targeted. A separate task should address the global forms.css issue (replacing 'bg-none!' with 'bg-color-transparent' or removing it entirely since background-image is set on the next line).

designDecisions.question: Will changing the query var break existing bookmarked/shared URLs with per_page_post=X?
designDecisions.recommendation: Yes, existing bookmarked URLs with old per_page_* params will no longer set per-page. This is acceptable because: (1) archive filter URLs are transient/session-specific and rarely bookmarked, (2) the old params will simply be ignored (no errors), (3) the default postsPerPage from the block attribute will apply as fallback. No migration or backward-compatibility shim is needed.
